political.s: EY (Ernst & Young) is a private partnership with no corporate PAC. A high-profile failed 2023 split ('Project Everest') between its audit and consulting arms drew significant regulatory and political attention.

charity.s: EY Foundation supports social enterprise and young people not in education, employment or training (NEET). The firm contributes tens of millions in pro bono services annually.

environment.s: EY committed to carbon negative by 2025 for its own operations and has SBTi-approved targets. The firm achieved carbon neutrality across Scope 1 and 2 in 2020.

labor.s: EY has faced criticism for high-pressure audit culture. In India, an EY employee death in 2024 was attributed by the family to overwork, sparking global attention to consulting work culture. Glassdoor ratings are average for Big Four.

dei.s: EY scored 100 on HRC CEI and has explicit targets for gender and ethnic diversity in leadership. The firm publishes a detailed annual diversity report.

guns.s: EY does not sell or manufacture firearms.

privacy.s: EY handles sensitive financial data for major corporations worldwide. In 2023, EY was affected by the MOVEit file transfer vulnerability, exposing some client data alongside thousands of other organizations globally.

execPay.s: EY is private; partner compensation not publicly disclosed. Average partner compensation approximately $750K.
